REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (414-416 Waller St, San Francisco, Ca)
Off Calendar Nov-04-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gary Cohen for and with Lisa Ferdinand. Francis Kelly for Eliska Ferdinand and Eugenia Barnes (Objectors). Tim Beresky for Gwendolyn Ferdinand.

The Court ordered the status on the filing of the receipts for the blocked account off calendar; receipts filed. The Court then addressed the issues with regard to the two percent commission, the desposit at the close of escrow, whether there was an objection to the sale, the issue raised in regard as to whether or not there was a relationship between the broker and prospective buyer. Mr. Beresky orally set forth the names of the buyers as Lisa Ferdinand and Willie Gilton, stated that Lisa Ferdinand is the sister of his client, that there is familiarity there, that the property was properly marketed for sale, and that the purchase price is comparable. Mr. Beresky also stated that his client hired someone to sell four properties, that this property had the lower commission because of the multiple sales, that his client knew that her sister was interested in the property, and that there was no relationship between broker and the buyer. Mr. Cohen stated that his client lives in and has a business in the property, that the objection made sense before the reappraisal, and that the property has been reappraised at a correct amount. Mr. Kelly presented argument in support of his opposition noting that it was a sweetheart deal and that the reappraisals weren't filed until the weekend. Mr. Cohen stated that the contract does show the second buyer. At the conclusion of argument, the Court stated that the sale would proceed. There being no overbids, the sale was confirmed at $730,000 and is subject to the terms and conditions set forth in the report of sale. No additional bond is required. Funds are to be placed in a blocked account. The Court granted the petition (subject to review of the written order).

Judge: Mary Wiss. Court Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.
